title: Implied odds — pot-odds adjustment for hidden draws (rake-agnostic, 100bb effective)
bodyMarkdown:
Pot odds alone reject most drawing-hand calls. Implied odds adjust the threshold by adding expected future winnings to the denominator.

Formula: required_equity = call_cost / (current_pot + call_cost + expected_future_winnings).

Equivalently: effective_pot_odds_threshold ≈ base_pot_odds_threshold × (current_pot + call_cost) / (current_pot + call_cost + expected_future_winnings).

Set-mining floor: pocket pair flops a set ~1-in-8 (~12% turn-or-river hit). Calling needs ~15:1 implied to break even on the set-mine alone.

Worked examples (rake-agnostic, 100bb effective):
- 9-out flush draw on flop, facing half-pot bet (base 25% threshold): unaided equity ≈ 35% turn-or-river → already calls without implied. With implied (expect 0.6× pot extra when hit), threshold drops further to ~18%.
- Pocket pair set-mining 100bb deep, opener bets 3bb after raise to 7bb (pot 17bb, call 4bb, base 19% threshold): unaided ≈ 12% — needs implied. Stack-off potential when set hits adds ~80bb of winnings → effective threshold ≈ 6%, set-mine call clearly +EV.

Reverse implied odds: top-pair weak-kicker on draw-heavy boards LOSES bets when hits — invert sign on expected future winnings.

See POKER_THEORY.md §1.5 (Pot Odds and Implied Odds) for the canonical derivation.
theoryCitation: POKER_THEORY.md §1.5 (Pot Odds and Implied Odds) — canonical implied-odds + reverse-implied-odds derivation.

assumptions.field: all 9-handed live cash and tournament fields; implied-odds derivation is field-invariant

atomicityJustification: Single derivation (implied odds adjustment to pot odds) presented as one card.
